title: High-Income DINK Couple: Accelerated Wealth Building
goals: Diversify concentrated employer stock RSU position
Accelerate wealth building toward early retirement (FIRE)
constraints: Above Roth IRA income limits ($240K MFJ threshold 2024)
RSU vesting creates concentrated single-stock risk
No taxable investment account despite large surplus
Complex tax situation with RSU income
key_risks: 32% of investable assets in single employer stock
David's human capital and investment correlated with same company
No estate plan — assets would pass by intestacy
Large cash balance earning below-market returns

planning_issues.description: Concentrated employer stock: 32% of investable portfolio in single company; correlated with David's employment
Backdoor Roth IRA not implemented; 401k contributions below maximum despite $350K income
$145,000 in cash savings above emergency fund earning 4.5% — opportunity cost vs long-term market returns
No wills, powers of attorney, or healthcare directives; no estate plan at $560K net worth

key_recommendations: Execute backdoor Roth IRA for both spouses ($7,000 each annually)
Open HSA accounts and maximize (triple tax advantage)
Systematic RSU diversification — sell at vesting, reinvest in index funds
Open taxable brokerage; invest $6,000–$8,000/month from surplus
follow_up_questions: Does David's 401k allow after-tax contributions (mega backdoor Roth)?
Planning to purchase a home in 3-5 years? (Affects liquid allocation)
What is the target retirement age — FIRE or conventional 65?
Has AMT exposure been modeled for any ISO stock options?
outcome_summary: Maximized both 401ks and backdoor Roth IRAs. Sold 60% of concentrated stock over 12 months. Taxable brokerage opened and grew to $180,000.
key_lesson: High-income dual-earner households with no children often have large untapped tax-advantaged space and concentrated stock risk. Structured contribution and diversification planning materially improves retirement wealth.
